components.blueprintMarkdown:
# Button blueprint

_Framework_: react

## Props
- `children`: `ReactNode`
- `size`: `'sm' | 'md' | 'lg'` _(optional)_
- `tone`: `'neutral' | 'brand' | 'danger'` _(optional)_
- `disabled`: `boolean` _(optional)_
- `loading`: `boolean` _(optional)_
- `onClick`: `() => void` _(optional, event)_

## Default args
- `children`: "Preview"
- `size`: "md"
- `tone`: "neutral"
- `disabled`: false
- `loading`: false

## Controls
- `size`: {"control": "radio", "options": ["sm", "md", "lg"]}
- `tone`: {"control": "inline-radio", "options": ["neutral", "brand", "danger"]}
- `onClick`: {"control": false, "useFn": true}

## Recommended stories
- Default (baseline)
- Large (decision)
- Brand (decision)
- Disabled (state)
- Loading (state)
- LongContent (boundary)

## Usage signals
- Matches found: 2 across 28 scanned files
- `tone` appears in 2 usage sites
- `disabled` appears in 1 usage sites
- `loading` appears in 1 usage sites
- `onClick` appears in 1 usage sites
- `size` appears in 1 usage sites
- Co-occurs: `disabled + loading` (1)
- Co-occurs: `disabled + tone` (1)
- Co-occurs: `loading + tone` (1)

## Composition stories
- InButtonUsage: inline parent context; fixtures/basic-button/ButtonUsage.tsx; size="lg", tone="brand"; bindings: onClick
- InButtonUsageButtonUsage: inline parent context; fixtures/basic-button/ButtonUsage.tsx; disabled=true, loading=true, tone="danger"

## Branch signals
- `loading`: state gate (1)

## Interaction stories
- ClickFlow: click the primary trigger -> assert onClick was called

## Accessibility stories
- BusyAndDisabledStates: Disabled semantics and busy messaging (WCAG 4.1.2, 3.3.2)

## Visual diff plan
- Harnesses: Chromatic, Playwright screenshot test
- Capture: Default, Large, Brand, Disabled, Loading, LongContent

## Notes
- Use fn() from storybook/test for event handlers and hide direct controls for them.
- Prefer one or two decision stories over rendering every enum combination.
- Bias stories toward prop combinations already used together in the app.
- Props that gate conditional branches deserve explicit state or boundary stories.
- Add at least one parent-context story so the component is reviewed where it actually ships.

components.blueprintMarkdown:
# Alert blueprint

_Framework_: react

## Props
- `title`: `string`
- `message`: `string`
- `tone`: `'info' | 'success' | 'danger'` _(optional)_
- `dismissible`: `boolean` _(optional)_
- `onDismiss`: `() => void` _(optional, event)_
- `className`: `string` _(optional, hidden)_

## Default args
- `title`: "Component title"
- `message`: "Helpful supporting text."
- `tone`: "info"
- `dismissible`: false

## Controls
- `tone`: {"control": "inline-radio", "options": ["info", "success", "danger"]}
- `onDismiss`: {"control": false, "useFn": true}
- `className`: {"control": false, "table": {"disable": true}}

## Recommended stories
- Default (baseline)
- Danger (decision)
- Dismissible (state)
- LongContent (boundary)

## Branch signals
- `dismissible`: state gate (1)

## Interaction stories
- DismissFlow: activate the dismiss button with mouse and keyboard -> assert the dismiss handler fires and focus remains sane

## Accessibility stories
- ScreenReaderSummary: Labeling and message announcement (WCAG 1.3.1, 4.1.2)
- KeyboardDismiss: Keyboard-only activation and focus order (WCAG 2.1.1, 2.4.3)

## Visual diff plan
- Harnesses: Chromatic, Playwright screenshot test
- Capture: Default, Danger, Dismissible, LongContent

## Notes
- Use fn() from storybook/test for event handlers and hide direct controls for them.
- Hide internal props from docs tables and controls: className.
- Prefer one or two decision stories over rendering every enum combination.
- Props that gate conditional branches deserve explicit state or boundary stories.

components.blueprintMarkdown:
# ThemeBadge blueprint

_Framework_: react

## Props
- `label`: `string`
- `theme`: `'light' | 'dark' | 'system'` _(optional)_
- `selected`: `boolean` _(optional)_
- `compact`: `boolean` _(optional)_
- `className`: `string` _(optional, hidden)_

## Default args
- `label`: "Ready"
- `theme`: "system"
- `selected`: false
- `compact`: false

## Controls
- `theme`: {"control": "inline-radio", "options": ["light", "dark", "system"]}
- `className`: {"control": false, "table": {"disable": true}}

## Recommended stories
- Default (baseline)
- Dark (decision)
- Light (decision)
- Selected (state)
- Compact (state)
- LongContent (boundary)

## Usage signals
- Matches found: 3 across 28 scanned files
- `label` appears in 3 usage sites
- `theme` appears in 3 usage sites
- `selected` appears in 2 usage sites
- `compact` appears in 1 usage sites
- Co-occurs: `label + theme` (3)
- Co-occurs: `label + selected` (2)
- Co-occurs: `selected + theme` (2)

## Composition stories
- InThemeBadgeUsage: inline parent context; fixtures/theme-badge/ThemeBadgeUsage.tsx; label="System", theme="system"
- InThemeBadgeUsageThemeBadgeUsage: inline parent context; fixtures/theme-badge/ThemeBadgeUsage.tsx; label="Light", selected=true, theme="light"
- InThemeBadgeUsageThemeBadgeUsage: inline parent context; fixtures/theme-badge/ThemeBadgeUsage.tsx; compact=true, label="Dark", selected=true, theme="dark"

## Accessibility stories
- FieldNarration: Visible labels and described-by relationships (WCAG 1.3.1, 3.3.1)

## Visual diff plan
- Harnesses: Chromatic, Playwright screenshot test
- Capture: Default, Dark, Light, Selected, Compact, LongContent

## Notes
- Hide internal props from docs tables and controls: className.
- Prefer one or two decision stories over rendering every enum combination.
- Bias stories toward prop combinations already used together in the app.
- Add at least one parent-context story so the component is reviewed where it actually ships.

components.name: StatusPill
components.componentSource: {label}
components.storySource:
<script module lang="ts">
  import { defineMeta } from '@storybook/addon-svelte-csf';

  import StatusPill from './StatusPill.svelte';

  const { Story } = defineMeta({
    title: 'Fixtures/Svelte/StatusPill',
    component: StatusPill,
    tags: ['autodocs'],
    args: {
      label: 'Synced',
      tone: 'neutral',
      selected: false,
      compact: false,
    },
    argTypes: {
      tone: {
        control: 'inline-radio',
        options: ['neutral', 'brand', 'danger'],
      },
    },
  });
</script>

<Story name="Default" />

<Story
  name="Brand"
  args={{
    tone: 'brand',
    label: 'Ready',
  }}
/>

<Story
  name="Danger"
  args={{
    tone: 'danger',
    label: 'Failed',
  }}
/>

<Story
  name="Selected"
  args={{
    selected: true,
  }}
/>

<Story
  name="Compact"
  args={{
    compact: true,
  }}
/>

notes: Prefer one or two decision stories over rendering every enum combination.
Capture the stable default story first, then branch states that change layout or density.
Keep local screenshot thresholds narrow when stories are token-aware or theme-aware.
